reasoning_summary: For live run 20260421_113859_bf16_gemm_v1_6668d21 at 46.366718 ms, the bounded PTX live-state trim is an unambiguous PASS_LOSS even though registers fell slightly from 200 to 198. Active warps stayed flat at 16.63%, barrier regressed from 5.47% to 5.95%, and the accepted base remains 20260421_111322_bf16_gemm_v1_f768e80 at 24.293376 ms. That means the next step should be a precise recovery to the accepted surface before trying another exploit, while preserving the richer NCU deltas as evidence. I keep a barrier-only PTX retime second because barrier did worsen on this probe, and a 256x128 salvage branch third so the queue does not collapse to only restore traffic.

family_audit.rationale: Round-3 PTX live-state trim produced a large measured loss; restoring the accepted surface is the only responsible next anchor.
Barrier worsened on the failed probe and remains the clearest next bounded exploit once the accepted surface is restored.

The exact bounded probe just measured a large loss, so this family should pause until the accepted base is re-established.
